text: When you first notice a bug in your code, the most important step is to identify exactly what is wrong. Instead of guessing, take a systematic approach: first, reproduce the error, then isolate the part of the code that causes it. Many programming tools, like debuggers and linters, can help you step through your code line by line. Another useful technique is logging, where you add print statements or use a logging library to track variable values as your program runs. By combining these strategies and staying patient, you can solve problems more efficiently and avoid wasting time on random changes.
